children.label: i
children.text: decrease the amount of coverage or require the payment of an additional amount as premiums if the insured engages in military service (except increases in premiums in individual term insurance based upon age); or

children.label: ii
children.text: limit or restrict coverage for any activity required by military service; and

children.label: B
children.text: is in force not less than 180 days before the date of the insured’s entry into military service and at the time of application under this subchapter.

children.heading: Premium
children.label: 2 Premium
children.text: The term “premium” means the amount specified in an insurance policy to be paid to keep the policy in force.

children.heading: Insured
children.label: 3 Insured
children.text: The term “insured” means a servicemember whose life is insured under a policy.

children.heading: Insurer
children.label: 4 Insurer
children.text: The term “insurer” includes any firm, corporation, partnership, association, or business that is chartered or authorized to provide insurance and issue contracts or policies by the laws of a State or the United States.
